hypothesis: For Covalent Triazine Framework substrates, reinforcement learning of a SMILES-based generative model using a composite reward that averages the expert filter score and a neural-network surrogate trained to predict DFT-computed electronic conductivity from 3D conformers will produce generated molecules whose mean DFT-computed electronic conductivity (evaluated on the top 100 candidates) is significantly greater (p < 0.05, two-sided test) than the mean conductivity of molecules produced by RL optimizing the expert-filter score alone.
